description: Manufactures semiconductor production equipment and precision measuring instruments. Core products: wafer probing machines (world #1, 46% global share), wafer dicing machines (~10% global share, #2 behind DISCO), CMP equipment, polish grinders, edge grinders, and precision dicing blades. Brand name: ACCRETECH. Founded 1949. Two segments: SPE (~75% of sales, ~113B JPY) and Metrology (~25%, ~37B JPY). Revenue FY2025/3: 150.5B JPY (+11.8% YoY). Q3 FY2026/3 (9M): revenue 113.0B JPY (+9.5% YoY), OP 20.9B JPY (+9.7% YoY). Revised FY2026/3 guidance: revenue 165.0B JPY (+9.6%), OP 32.0B JPY (+7.7%). Mid-term plan (FY2028/3): revenue 180B JPY, OP 45B JPY, OPM 24.3%. China sales ratio: 34% (#1 market). Joint die-level prober development with Advantest (Dec 2025) for AI/HPC. Hub blade JV with Asahi Diamond Industrial (Jan 2025). Fortress balance sheet: equity ratio 73.2%, virtually debt-free. 28 subsidiaries in 18 countries. 2,767 employees.

majorClients: Powertech Technology (CMP-0069) - dicing, probing (Taiwan memory OSAT)
Hua Hong Semiconductor (CMP-0070) - CMP, probing (China #2 foundry)

Nexchip (CMP-0083) - probing, dicing, CMP (China #3 foundry, 12-inch DDIC)

partners: Advantest (CMP-0005) - joint development of die-level prober for AI/HPC testing, addressing heat management in 2.5D/3D packaged AI chips (announced Dec 2025)
Asahi Diamond Industrial - hub blade JV for dicing consumables, mass production by FY2027 (MOU Jan 2025)
AA Diamond Technology - JV for diamond technology in semiconductor applications (May 2025)
keyShareholderContext: Master Trust Bank of Japan: 17.84% (custodial, largest holder)
_externalRelationships.notes: Together with DISCO, controls 90% of global dicing market. World #1 prober maker (46% share vs TEL 27%). China is 34% of sales — largest single market. 28 subsidiaries in 18 countries. Regional offices: Accretech Korea (Dongtan), Accretech Taiwan (Hsinchu), Accretech China (Shanghai + 8 offices), Accretech Singapore, Accretech Malaysia (Selangor/Penang/Melaka/JB). Wave 5 analysis: 35 total identified clients across 7 countries, 15 competitors. Chinese domestic substitution is #1 strategic risk (CMP >50% lost, probing under pressure from Sidea 25.7% domestic share, dicing still defensible). Korean localization via SEMES/SEMICS/KCTech is #2 risk. Export controls restrict sales to Entity List companies but accelerate domestic substitution. Stock has rallied 86% over 52 weeks driven by AI enthusiasm and semi cycle recovery. Product quality issues ('defect countermeasures') weighing on FY2026/3 net income. DISCO operates at significantly higher margins (>30% vs ~20%). Total shareholders: 15,109. Shares outstanding (ex-treasury): ~40.58M.

_metadata.dataSource: ACCRETECH IR (accretech.com), Yahoo Finance, Investing.com, MarketScreener, StockAnalysis, SimplyWallSt, Mordor Intelligence, SemiAnalysis, Gabelli Research, Nikkei, Chemical Daily, industry reports, Japanese/Chinese/Korean/English language research
_metadata.notes: Deep research update 2026-02-23. Q3 FY2026/3 (9M to Dec 2025): revenue 113.0B JPY (+9.5% YoY), OP 20.9B JPY (+9.7% YoY), NI 14.1B JPY (-21.9% YoY due to one-time items). FY2026/3 revised guidance (Feb 2026): revenue 165.0B JPY, OP 32.0B JPY, NI 21.5B JPY. Order backlog stable at 87.4B JPY. Stock at 52-week highs (~16,895 JPY), up 86% over 52 weeks. Trailing P/E expanded to ~31.8x, P/B to 3.79x. Mid-term plan FY2028/3 targets: 180B revenue, 45B OP, 24.3% OPM. Advantest JV (Dec 2025) for die-level probers for AI/HPC. Asahi Diamond JV (Jan 2025) for hub blades. Foreign ownership at 32.23% (non-individual). Dividend yield ~1.3% at current price.
